events.title: Commemorative Air Force Museum Tours
events.description: The CAF-Utah Wing represents living history and is unique among aviation museums. The Utah Wing houses a significant collection of aircraft, authentic World War II artifacts, and women in aviation memorabilia. Items on display in our museum include Allied country uniforms, as well as armament, photographs, weapons, and equipment from both Allied and Axis countries. The Utah Wing of the CAF is a self-supporting, all-volunteer, non-profit, tax-exempt organization registered in Utah. Funds to acquire, restore, maintain and operate aircraft are derived from members' dues, contributions, aircraft sponsors, and fundraising events. All donations, including dues and aircraft sponsorships, are used to support the Wing's museum and to restore, operate, and maintain our Ghost Squadron.

He looks at himself not as a guitar player, but an artist who utilizes the guitar and other forms to create an inspiring, healing, and contemplative experience for himself and listeners. Alternate tunings and intuitive playing have contributed immensely by shaping the instrumental and lyrical music that comes through him. He has a background in Integrative Health and is currently studying Music and Recording Arts Technology which is taking his creative possibilities to new depths and heights. He has performed at festivals in Thailand, Costa Rica, and the East Coast (US), as well as in yoga classes, local gigs, and workshops where he incorporates live music, meditation, group energy healing and writing into one experience. events.title: Longevity, Simplified: What Actually Moves the Needle for Your Health
events.description: Onyx Club is proud to present a focused conversation on what truly drives long-term health, performance, and longevity. Join Dr. Timothy Duerler of Ovation Private Health and Connor Darnbrough, Co-founder of The Smart Fit Method for a focused discussion on the key drivers of longevity and health optimization. In this session they will break down the Four Pillars of Longevity – sleep, exercise, nutrition, and stress – and how they translate into real, measurable outcomes in your daily life. This talk is about understanding what actually works and how to apply it in a realistic, sustainable way. You’ll learn: How to focus on the key drivers that have the biggest impact on your health and longevity How advanced testing and diagnostics (lab work, body composition, VO₂ max, and more) can personalize your approach What data actually matters and how tracking the right metrics can improve performance and long-term health How to implement these principles within a busy, real-life schedule Connor will also share how these concepts come to life through the Smart Fit Method—a highly efficient, science-based training approach built from his own journey of injury, recovery, and rebuilding strength. After experiencing severe physical setbacks early in life, beginning his journey as a professional skier, he developed a system focused on helping others recover, perform, and extend their health span. Dr. Duerler brings over two decades of experience in family medicine, now focused on proactive, prevention-based care. His work centers on helping individuals preserve vitality, function, and cognitive performance through personalized, data-informed strategies. Together, they offer a grounded, practical perspective on how to take control of your health without overcomplicating it. The evening will include a brief presentation, followed by Q&A and time to connect with light bites and refreshments. Perks Attendees receive 15% off at the Overlook Restaurant at Black Rock Mountain Resort ...

events.title: Bike ‘n’ Botany
events.description: Join us for a leisurely pedal to learn about common noxious weeds of the Snyderville Basin. We’ll start at the Swaner EcoCenter then ride the paved and packed gravel paths toward Willow Creek Park, stopping along the way to identify and discuss noxious weed species. Need to borrow a bike? Indicate this at registration and we’ll reach out with details about how to access a bike for free from Summit Bike Share for the ride.

events.description: Live PC Give PC will take place on FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2026! HELP SHAPE PARK CITY’S FUTURE IN A SINGLE DAY. Every November, Live PC Give PC brings our community together through LivePCGivePC.org for 24 hours of extreme generosity. Children break open their piggy banks. Young people dip into their savings. Businesses kick in matching grants. With everyone in the community giving whatever they can, we’ve raised millions of dollars for local nonprofits. It’s how we’re working together to preserve the place we love—so it will continue to thrive for generations to come.

events.title: American Indian Services Native Market Days
events.description: Native Market Days | July 10–11, 2026 Canyons Village | Park City, Utah Native Market Days is a signature cultural experience hosted by American Indian Services, celebrating Native art, culture, fashion, dance, storytelling, and community. Held at Canyons Village in Park City, this event brings together Native artists, performers, designers, families, community members, and visitors in a vibrant setting that honors creativity, connection, and cultural expression. This two-day event will feature a Native Market, hoop dance performances, an art competition and judging experience, community gatherings, and a Native Fashion Show. Sponsors and partners are invited to help elevate Native voices, support Native artists and entrepreneurs, and align their brand with a meaningful cultural experience rooted in respect, representation, and celebration.
